segments.s009-g17: برفت او is a fronted past-tense verb phrase: برفت is an archaic/poetic past form meaning 'he went/ departed' with the subject او following it (verb-before-subject for emphasis). و links the next clause. این نامه ناگفته ماند is literal SOV for 'this letter remained unsaid': ناگفته is a participial adjective formed with the negative/participle prefix نا- + گفته ('said') meaning 'unsaid, untold', and ماند is the past-tense verb 'remained/was left'.
segments.s009-g18: چنان here is an adverb 'thus/so' introducing the result clause. بخت is the noun 'fortune/luck' and بیدار is an adjectival participle 'awake/alert' modifying بخت; او is the possessor 'his'. خفته is another participial adjective 'asleep', and ماند is the past verb 'remained' — together the phrase says that, paradoxically, his apparently 'awake' fortune nevertheless 'remained asleep'. Word order is typical poetic: adverb → noun phrase → predicate.
segments.s009-g19: الهی is a vocative/exclamatory invocation 'O God!'. عفو کن is an imperative clause where the noun عفو ('forgiveness') combines with the imperative verb کن ('do') to mean 'grant forgiveness' or 'forgive'. گناه ورا is the direct object: گناه 'sin' with the enclitic/poetic object marker ورا ('to him' / 'him'), placed after the noun.
segments.s009-g20: بیفزای is an imperative 'add/increase' (a prayerful request). در حشر is a prepositional temporal phrase 'in/on the Resurrection (Day of Judgment)', where حشر means 'resurrection/assembly'. جاه ورا is the object 'his rank/status' with ورا again marking the person 'to him'; the verb governs this object and the locative phrase.
segments.s009-g1: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing a temporal clause. از دفتر این داستان‌ها بسی is a prepositional/nominal phrase: از + دفتر 'from the book', این داستان‌ها 'these tales', and بسی here is a quantifier meaning 'many/much' (poetic placement of بسی after the noun). The whole segment sets the time/setting for the action in the next segment.
segments.s009-g2: همی‌خواند is a classical-imperfective marker (kept on doing / would recite) used here to express habitual past action; the verb appears before its noun subject in poetic word order. خواننده is the agent 'the reciter'. بر هر کسی is a prepositional phrase 'to/for every person' specifying the audience.
segments.s009-g3: جهان functions as a collective subject 'the world' (i.e. the people). دل نهاده is a perfect-participial construction 'having placed (its) heart' or 'has set (its) heart' (نهاده = placed), and بدین داستان (poetic contraction of به + این) is the prepositional object 'on this story'. Together this is a stative/result clause.
segments.s009-g4: همان is a demonstrative 'those / the same', introducing the groups who are affected. بخردان is the plural noun 'the wise (people)'. نیز و هم is a pair of adverbial particles 'also / likewise' used for emphasis and poetic balance. راستان is an adjective/noun 'the upright / righteous' paired with بخردان to mean 'both the wise and the righteous alike.' The sequence is nominal listing with emphatic particles.
segments.s009-g5: جوانی is an indefinite subject 'a youth / a young man' (the -ی form marks indefiniteness). بیامد is the simple past 'came'. گشاده‌زبان is a compound adjective 'broad-tongued' = 'eloquent / frank' that follows the subject and characterizes him.
segments.s009-g6: This is a verbless descriptive phrase modifying the youth: سخن گفتن 'speaking' here is an infinitival/masdar construction meaning 'his speaking' or 'to speak', خوب 'well' qualifies it (i.e. 'good at speaking'), و links to طبع روان where طبع 'nature/temperament' + روان 'flowing/ fluent' describes his natural disposition. The whole gives two complementary qualities: skill in speech and a fluent temperament.
segments.s009-g7: This line uses inverted/poetic word order: به شعر آرم is a first-person clause 'I will put (it) into poetry/into verse' (آرم is a classical 1st-person form meaning 'I put / I render'); این نامه را is the definite object 'this letter' (را marks the object); گفت من places the reporting verb 'said I' after the quotation-clause — standard poetic inversion for emphasis.
segments.s009-g8: از او شادمان شد is a narrative result clause: از او = 'because of him / on his account', شادمان 'happy' (predicate adjective), شد 'became' (past of شدن). دل انجمن is the subject 'the heart(s) of the assembly' (poetic omission of the ezāfe linking دل and انجمن). Together: 'the assembly's heart became glad because of him.'
segments.s009-g9: 'جوانیش را' places 'his youth' as the topical/definite phrase (را is the object/topic marker). The clause 'خوی بد یار بود' uses یار بود ('was a companion') as the copular construction: خوی بد (bad disposition) is described as his companion — word order is poetic/inverted, with the copula بود in the past at the end.
segments.s009-g10: ابا بد is a noun (ابا, 'reluctance/aversion') modified by بد ('bad'). 'همیشه' is the adverb 'always' and 'به پیکار بود' is an adjectival/adverbial phrase 'was toward/at fighting' where بود is the past copula. The whole means that a bad reluctance/cowardice was continually opposed to (or at odds with) combat.
segments.s009-g11: This is a poetic inversion: the adverb ناگاه 'suddenly' and the verb phrase 'تاختن کرد' ('did charge/ran') frame the clause while the subject مرگ ('Death') appears at the end. 'بر او' is the prepositional phrase 'upon him/against him', and کرد is the past light verb used with the infinitive/masdar تاختن to form a past action.
segments.s009-g12: نهادش is the past of نهاد (here 'placed/put') with the clitic -ش = 'him' as its object; the implied subject is Death from the previous clause. 'به سر بر' means 'upon the head' (به + سر with the emphatic/locative بر), and 'یکی تیره ترگ' is the indefinite noun phrase 'one dark shred' (تیره = dark/ominous, ترگ = rag/shred) placed on his head.
segments.s009-g13: بدان is an archaic/compressed particle meaning 'by that' or 'thereby' (ب + آن). 'خوی بد' = 'bad disposition'. 'جان شیرین' is an idiomatic/poetic object 'sweet (dear) life' (جان = life/soul, شیرین = sweet/pleasant). بداد here functions as a past verb 'gave/caused' (used idiomatically to indicate yielding or losing one's life), with a strongly negative sense in context.
segments.s009-g14: نبد is an archaic negative/existential verb meaning 'there was not' (variant of نبود). 'از جوانیش یک روز شاد' is the source/measure phrase: 'from his youth [there was] one day happy' — i.e., 'not one happy day of his youth existed.' Word order places the negated verb first for emphasis.
segments.s009-g15: یکایک is an adverb meaning 'one by one' or 'each in turn'. 'از او' marks the person affected ('on him' / 'of him') and بخت‌برگشته (literally 'fortune-turned') is a compound adjective meaning 'ill-fated' or 'fortune reversed'. شد is the past auxiliary 'became/happened': together, 'one by one ill fortune befell him.'
segments.s009-g16: 'به دست' is the agent/means phrase 'by the hand of' (here 'by'), with دست used metonymically for power/agency. 'یکی بنده' = 'a certain servant/slave.' 'بر کشته شد' is a classical/periphrastic passive: کشته ('killed') plus شد ('became') yields 'was killed'; the particle بر before the verb is a classical element adding direction/emphasis in such constructions.

sentences.s009-s9: He departed, and this letter remained unsaid; thus his fortune, though awake, stayed asleep.
sentences.s009-s10: O God, pardon his sin, and at the Resurrection increase his rank.
sentences.s009-s1: When the reciter would read many of these tales from the book to every person,
sentences.s009-s2: the world had set its heart on this story, both the wise and the upright alike,
sentences.s009-s3: a young man came, eloquent of tongue, good at speaking and of a fluent nature;
sentences.s009-s4: I said, 'I will put this letter into verse,' and the assembly's heart became glad because of him.
sentences.s009-s5: A bad disposition was his companion in youth; a cowardly reluctance was always at odds with battle.
sentences.s009-s6: Suddenly Death charged upon him; it set (placed) upon his head a dark shred.
sentences.s009-s7: By that bad habit he surrendered his sweet life; not even one day of his youth was joyful.
sentences.s009-s8: One by one ill fortune turned against him; he was slain by a certain servant.
